input.prompt:
I have a Blazor Web App with per-page Interactive Server mode (not global).
The MainLayout renders statically (no @rendermode attribute on it).

I need to build an app-wide theme system with these requirements:

1. A theme service that holds the current theme (Light/Dark/System) and
   a user's preferred accent color (string). Any component on any page
   should be able to read AND change these values.

2. When ANY component changes the theme (e.g., a toggle in the sidebar
   on one page), all OTHER components consuming the theme must immediately
   update — including components in the static layout area (like a theme
   indicator in the header).

3. I also want a "user preferences" panel on a /settings page where the
   user can change both theme and accent color. Saving here must update
   the header theme indicator AND any themed components on any page.

4. Each user (circuit) must have independent theme state — one user
   switching to dark mode must not affect another user.

5. When navigating between pages (enhanced nav), the theme choice must
   persist without page reload.

What's the best pattern for sharing this state app-wide across all
pages and components, given the per-page interactivity setup?

skillInvocation.description: Share state between components that don't have a direct parent-child parameter relationship, using cascading values, scoped services with change events, or CascadingValueSource via DI. USE WHEN the user needs a CascadingParameter or CascadingValue that works across render mode boundaries, a shopping cart or notification count accessible from multiple pages, a theme or user preference cascaded app-wide, or when components in different parts of the tree must react when shared data changes. Also USE WHEN cascading values aren't reaching interactive children in per-page interactivity mode, or when the user needs to understand scoped vs singleton service lifetime for state on Blazor Server. DO NOT USE for direct parent-child parameter passing or EventCallback (see author-component), for persisting state across prerender-to-interactive transitions (see support-prerendering), or for service abstractions for data fetching in Auto/WebAssembly (see fetch-and-send-data).

data.reasoningText:
Now I understand the setup. Per-page Interactive Server mode, static MainLayout. The key challenge:

1. `<CascadingValue>` in the static MainLayout won't reach interactive children — it doesn't cross render mode boundaries.
2. Need `CascadingValueSource<T>` registered in DI for the theme to cross boundaries.
3. Need a scoped service for mutable state that components can both read and write.

The best pattern here combines:
- A **scoped `ThemeService`** with an `Action OnChange` event for mutable read/write state
- A **`CascadingValueSource<ThemeState>`** registered in DI for cascading the theme to all components (crossing render mode boundaries)


- The service will update the cascading value source via `NotifyChangedAsync` whenever the theme changes

Now I'm building out the implementation: defining the `ThemeState` model, creating the scoped `ThemeService` that manages state and notifies subscribers, registering both in the DI container, then building the UI components (`ThemeIndicator` for the header, `ThemeToggle` for interactive pages, and a full settings panel) and updating MainLayout to display the theme indicator.

data.content:
Build succeeds with zero errors/warnings. Here's a summary of everything that was added:

---

**Architecture — two mechanisms working together:**

| Piece | Role |
|-------|------|
| `CascadingValueSource<ThemeState>` (DI) | Crosses render-mode boundaries — the static header indicator AND interactive pages all receive updates via `[CascadingParameter]` |
| `ThemeService` (scoped) | Mutable read/write API. Components inject it to **change** the theme. It calls `NotifyChangedAsync()` on the cascading source so all subscribers re-render |

**Files created/modified:**

| File | What |
|------|------|
| `Services/ThemeState.cs` | Immutable record: `Theme` enum (Light/Dark/System) + `AccentColor` |
| `Services/ThemeService.cs` | Scoped service — `SetThemeAsync`, `SetAccentColorAsync`, `SetAllAsync` |
| `Program.cs` | Registers `CascadingValueSource<ThemeState>` (`isFixed: false`) + `AddScoped<ThemeService>` |
| `Layout/ThemeIndicator.razor` | Read-only badge in the header — uses `[CascadingParameter]` only |
| `Layout/ThemeToggle.razor` | Interactive button in sidebar — injects `ThemeService` to cycle themes |
| `Pages/Settings.razor` | Full preferences panel — theme buttons + accent color picker |
| `NavMenu.razor` | Added Settings link + ThemeToggle in sidebar |
| `MainLayout.razor` | Added `<ThemeIndicator />` in top row |

**Why this works for your constraints:**

1. **Per-page interactivity** — `CascadingValueSource<T>` is DI-based, not component-tree-based, so it crosses the static↔interactive boundary.
2. **Immediate cross-component updates** — `NotifyChangedAsync()` re-renders every `[CascadingParameter]` subscriber (header indicator updates when settings page changes theme).
3. **Per-circuit isolation** — `AddScoped` = one instance per SignalR circuit. Users don't share state.
4. **Persists across enhanced nav** — scoped services survive page transitions within the same circuit.
